title: RUN-0002 temperature selection
one_line: Neo switches into low-temperature normalizer / validator mode for the R14 pass.
this_is: a role swap: Neo validates while Phi/Fable5 previously explored
a bridge toward source audit, math skeletons, and Colab tests
this_is_not: not a new ontology
not a proof of R14
not a literature review
not a final verdict

decision.reason: The project needs alternating hot/cold passes to prevent charisma from becoming evidence.
decision.next_step: Use this run as RUN-0002 baseline for Opus/next-agent audit.

next_step_idea: Route this ledger to the next agent as audit input, not as doctrine.
notes: Chosen temperature: 0.25–0.35 equivalent: compress, classify, forbid overclaim.

title: R14 synthesis probe
one_line: R14 is reframed as Interference Render / Projection Invariants, not as a claim that reality literally is an interference render.
this_is: a synthesis probe across zeta statistics, explicit formulas, caustics, tensor networks, moonshine, Sorkin/Born tests, and coarse-graining
a map of computable projection-like invariants
this_is_not: not an origin theory
not a claim that the universe literally renders from a hidden source
not proof that probability is only brightness accounting
not publication-ready

decision.reason: The framing is useful only if each support is split into theorem/empirical/metaphor and tested separately.
decision.next_step: Do not publish R14 synthesis before R14-A and R14-C survive source audit + notebook.

next_step_idea: Run R14-A first because it is the cheapest path to math + compute.

title: R14-A · Zeta sine-kernel anchor
one_line: After unfolding, zeta-zero pair correlations are expected to follow the GUE sine-kernel form 1 - sinc²; R14 may visualize this as diffraction-like, but only after source audit.
this_is: a claim about unfolded correlations of zeros
this_is_not: not proof of literal diffraction
not proof of an interference ontology
not proof of Riemann hypothesis
not evidence that primes are consciously rendered

primary_sources_needed.exact_title_or_best_known_name: The pair correlation of zeros of the zeta function
primary_sources_needed.doi_or_arxiv_if_known: conference proceedings / Proc. Symp. Pure Math. 24 (verify)
primary_sources_needed.acceptable_substitute: A modern review of Montgomery pair correlation with exact sine-kernel statement
primary_sources_needed.why_needed: Primary source for the pair-correlation conjecture and 1 - (sin pi u / pi u)^2 form.

primary_sources_needed.exact_title_or_best_known_name: On the distribution of spacings between zeros of the zeta function
primary_sources_needed.acceptable_substitute: Odlyzko zero tables and papers on high zeros
primary_sources_needed.why_needed: Empirical/numerical support and data for pair-correlation computations.

primary_sources_needed.exact_title_or_best_known_name: Random matrices and the statistical theory of energy levels / sine kernel references
primary_sources_needed.acceptable_substitute: Forrester or Mehta textbook chapter on GUE sine-kernel correlations
primary_sources_needed.why_needed: Exact RMT comparison: GUE bulk pair correlation and sine kernel.

decision.reason: Cheapest bridge from hot synthesis to real math + compute.
decision.next_step: Request primary sources/data and build Colab spec.

next_step_idea: Ask Den for Montgomery/Odlyzko references or usable zero tables.

title: R14-A NULL
one_line: If unfolded zeta-zero correlations do not fit the sine-kernel baseline within expected finite-size and unfolding errors, the R14-A anchor is killed or demoted.
this_is: a pre-registered kill condition for the zeta-kernel notebook
this_is_not: not a claim that failure disproves Riemann-zero/RMT literature globally
not a universal kill for all of R14
null_cases: Failure to reproduce sine-kernel correlation after correct unfolding and sufficient sample size.
Strong dependence on arbitrary unfolding choices beyond accepted error.

decision.reason: Needs data and method before verdict.
decision.next_step: Implement with known zero table first; treat data/method mismatch as inconclusive, not dead.

next_step_idea: Write the notebook using a published zero table before importing larger datasets.

title: R14-A Colab spec · zeta kernel
one_line: Minimal notebook: load zeros → unfold → compute pair correlation / spacing statistics → compare with 1 - sinc² / sine-kernel baseline.
this_is: a small-compute visualization and reproduction task
this_is_not: not a proof of Montgomery conjecture
not a proof of R14 synthesis
not a substitute for source audit

inputs: published zero table or Odlyzko sample
steps: Load imaginary parts gamma_n of zeros on critical line
Unfold using local mean density N(T) derivative or supplied unfolded data
Compute pair correlation histogram / nearest-neighbor spacing as baseline shadow
Report finite-size residuals and sensitivity to bin width/unfolding

requested_files.name: Riemann zeta zero table, preferably Odlyzko high zeros or a small verified sample
requested_files.why: needed to run R14-A without scraping unreliable sources

decision.reason: Compute is simple once data/source are provided.
decision.next_step: Den brings zero table or authoritative source.

next_step_idea: Make this the first notebook after source audit.

title: R14-B · primes from zero waves
one_line: The explicit formula expresses prime-counting functions through contributions from zeta zeros; this supports a wave/interference visualization but not a literal ontology.
this_is: a theorem-backed relation between primes and zeros
this_is_not: not proof that primes are produced by physical waves
not proof of hidden source render
not a claim that truncated sums exactly locate primes without error

primary_sources_needed.exact_title_or_best_known_name: Riemann explicit formula for psi(x) / prime counting
primary_sources_needed.acceptable_substitute: Edwards, Riemann’s Zeta Function; Ivić; Ingham; Lagarias explicit formula paper
primary_sources_needed.why_needed: Exact statement connecting primes/prime counting to sums over nontrivial zeros.

primary_sources_needed.exact_title_or_best_known_name: The Riemann Hypothesis / explicit formula exposition
primary_sources_needed.acceptable_substitute: Any rigorous accessible source with explicit formula and convergence caveats
primary_sources_needed.why_needed: Need hypotheses, truncation errors, and what can be safely visualized.

decision.reason: Strong mathematics, but more subtle numerically than R14-A.
decision.next_step: Do after R14-A or use as companion figure.

next_step_idea: Use explicit formula only after theorem skeleton clarifies smoothing/truncation.

title: R14-B NULL
one_line: If truncated explicit-formula reconstructions do not localize prime-related peaks better than a matched baseline under documented truncation/smoothing, the notebook claim is demoted.
this_is: a kill/demotion condition for the visualization claim
this_is_not: not a kill of the explicit formula theorem
not a kill of Riemann-zero theory
null_cases: Peak localization score near primes is no better than baseline random phases or smoothed density model.
Visualization depends on cherry-picked truncation/window without documented error behavior.

decision.reason: Requires careful theorem skeleton before compute.
decision.next_step: Define metric and baseline before running.

next_step_idea: Ask a math-pass to define the exact smoothed function to reconstruct.

title: R14-B Colab spec · explicit formula shadow
one_line: Notebook: reconstruct a smoothed prime-counting signal from a finite set of zeros and compare localization metrics against baselines.
this_is_not: not exact prime prediction
not discovery of primes from scratch
not proof of any physical interference model

steps: Choose psi(x) or smoothed prime-counting target
Compare peaks/oscillations to prime positions or prime-counting error

decision.reason: Needs MATH_R14_B first or it will become a pretty lie.
decision.next_step: Create theorem skeleton before coding.

next_step_idea: Do not code before source audit.

title: R14-C · finite caustic alphabet
one_line: Generic caustics have a finite stable local alphabet under specified mapping dimensions; this is a strong 3D projection-invariant candidate.
this_is: a theorem-grade / controlled-math anchor after hypotheses are specified
an example of finite grammar of projection singularities
this_is_not: not proof that all reality is interference
not proof that every 3D pattern is a caustic
not valid without specifying mapping dimension and stability conditions

primary_sources_needed.exact_title_or_best_known_name: Catastrophe Optics: Morphologies of Caustics and their Diffraction Patterns
primary_sources_needed.acceptable_substitute: Berry caustics review with canonical diffraction catastrophes
primary_sources_needed.why_needed: Primary source for stable caustic/catastrophe alphabet and diffraction patterns.

primary_sources_needed.exact_title_or_best_known_name: Stable mappings and singularities / Catastrophe theory
primary_sources_needed.acceptable_substitute: Any rigorous singularity theory reference for fold/cusp classifications
review on caustics in gravitational lensing
primary_sources_needed.why_needed: Cross-scale empirical/theoretical bridge for optical and gravitational caustics.

decision.reason: Good because it has theorem-like classification plus visual small compute.
decision.next_step: Request Berry/Upstill or equivalent; then write MATH_R14_C.

next_step_idea: Make R14-C the second compute target after R14-A.

title: R14-C NULL
one_line: If generic phase-screen or lensing-like simulations produce stable caustic singularities outside the expected catastrophe alphabet under matching hypotheses, the R14-C anchor is killed or narrowed.
this_is: a kill condition for the finite-alphabet claim under its hypotheses
this_is_not: not a claim that all unusual singularities kill catastrophe theory
not applicable outside the theorem’s dimensional/stability conditions
null_cases: Stable, reproducible singularity outside fold/cusp/etc. appears in a generic perturbation class where theorem predicts only known forms.
Measured scaling exponents fail canonical Airy/fold behavior under controlled setup.

decision.reason: Requires exact theorem hypotheses before simulation is meaningful.
decision.next_step: Source audit first; simulation second.

next_step_idea: Clarify allowed alphabet by dimension before any notebook.

title: R14-C Colab spec · caustic alphabet
one_line: Generate random phase/lensing maps, detect generic caustics, classify fold/cusp, and measure canonical scaling where possible.
this_is: a test of projection singularity grammar
this_is_not: not a proof of catastrophe theory
not an ontological claim
not valid without source-defined detection criteria

steps: Map source grid to image grid or compute intensity proxy
Classify fold/cusp candidates using local geometry
Optionally fit Airy/fold scaling in 1D slice

decision.reason: Compute is feasible; exact classification must come from source audit.
decision.next_step: Den brings Berry/Upstill or Petters reference.

next_step_idea: Use this as first visual blog demo if math survives.

title: R14-D · tensor networks as scale render
one_line: Tensor networks/MERA and holographic reconstruction support a scale/RG reading of emergent geometry in specific settings, not a universal proof that spacetime is literally rendered.
this_is_not: not a Colab-first target
not proof of CCT ontology
not general spacetime reconstruction outside the controlled AdS/CFT/tensor-network settings

primary_sources_needed.exact_title_or_best_known_name: Holographic derivation of entanglement entropy from AdS/CFT
primary_sources_needed.why_needed: Primary anchor for entanglement entropy as area/minimal surface.

primary_sources_needed.exact_title_or_best_known_name: Building up spacetime with quantum entanglement
primary_sources_needed.why_needed: Entanglement as space-gluing interpretation with caveats.

decision.reason: Important but too broad; can easily overclaim.
decision.next_step: Route to R03/R06 source audit later.

next_step_idea: Do not activate until R14-A/C have produced notebooks.

title: R14-E · moonshine as resonance figure
one_line: Monstrous moonshine is theorem-grade structure linking modular functions, Monster representations, and VOA/CFT-like constructions; R14 may call it resonance only as interpretation.
this_is: a reminder that deep coincidences can become structure
this_is_not: not proof of hidden source ontology
not proof that Monster is literally an interference node
not a first compute target

primary_sources_needed.exact_title_or_best_known_name: Monstrous Moonshine and Monstrous Lie Superalgebras
primary_sources_needed.acceptable_substitute: Borcherds Fields Medal lecture / authoritative moonshine proof exposition

primary_sources_needed.exact_title_or_best_known_name: Vertex operator algebras and the Monster
primary_sources_needed.why_needed: Construction of Monster module / resonant medium language needs exact translation.

authors: Terry Gannon or modern review authors
primary_sources_needed.acceptable_substitute: survey separating theorem, conjecture, and physics interpretation
primary_sources_needed.why_needed: Needed to avoid overclaiming CFT/string interpretation.

decision.reason: Strong but not necessary for first math/compute cycle.
decision.next_step: Use later in R11 / coincidences as tunnels.

next_step_idea: Keep in theorem shelf, not first notebook.

title: R14-F · Sorkin parameter as kill switch
one_line: If robust third-order interference exists beyond accepted corrections, Born-level pairwise-interference framing is killed or must be generalized.
this_is: a strong falsification gate for pairwise/Born-rule render language
this_is_not: not an experiment we can directly perform easily
not proof that pairwise interference is the whole universe
not usable without loophole/correction audit

primary_sources_needed.why_needed: Defines higher-order interference and κ-like parameters.

primary_sources_needed.exact_title_or_best_known_name: Ruling out multi-order interference in quantum mechanics / triple-slit tests
primary_sources_needed.acceptable_substitute: experimental tests of Born rule / higher-order interference

primary_sources_needed.exact_title_or_best_known_name: Non-classical paths in quantum interference / loophole corrections
primary_sources_needed.acceptable_substitute: papers discussing triple-slit loopholes and nonclassical path corrections
primary_sources_needed.why_needed: Prevents overclaiming κ as direct Born-rule violation without corrections.

decision.reason: Important kill switch, but high risk of citation slippage.
decision.next_step: Source audit Sorkin/Sinha and correction literature before any claim.

next_step_idea: Make a literature-only mini-pass, not a Colab-first target.

title: R14-F NULL
one_line: A reproducible nonzero third-order interference parameter, after correcting for nonclassical paths and experimental loopholes, kills strict pairwise/Born-rule render framing.
this_is_not: not currently an observed result in this project
not a claim that existing experiments already killed QM
not source-audited yet
null_cases: κ remains robustly nonzero under the accepted definition and correction model.
Multiple independent experiments reproduce nonzero higher-order interference beyond uncertainty.

decision.reason: The kill-case is conceptually clean but source details matter.
decision.next_step: Ask next agent to audit the experimental status and caveats.

next_step_idea: Request primary Sorkin and Sinha-style papers.

title: R14-G · irreversible render / coarse-graining
one_line: Generic coarse-graining is many-to-one; R14 can treat render as irreversible only where a specific coarse-graining map loses distinguishable micro-information.
this_is_not: not proof that all perception is irreversible in the same way
not proof that reverse engineering is impossible in every constrained case
not a metaphysical ban on all reconstruction

primary_sources_needed.exact_title_or_best_known_name: Information bottleneck / coarse-graining and lost information
authors: Tishby et al. or modern review
primary_sources_needed.why_needed: Quantify irreversible compression in toy models.

decision.reason: Good toy computation, but less glamorous; useful for discipline.
decision.next_step: Do after zeta/caustics or if ontology tries to overgrow.

next_step_idea: Define toy map with known preimage degeneracy and compute lost bits.

title: R14-G Colab spec · coarse-graining loss
one_line: Toy notebook: define many microstates, coarse-grain to macrostates, compute preimage sizes and lost bits; optionally compare with invertible map control.
this_is_not: not a universal proof of irreversibility
not an RG theorem
not a deep physics simulation

steps: Define microstate space and probability distribution

decision.reason: Could run immediately but does not unlock source-heavy R14-A/C claims.
decision.next_step: Keep as discipline notebook.

title: Collision · diffraction-like vs literal diffraction ontology
one_line: The sine-kernel / sinc² visual analogy must not be promoted into literal diffraction ontology without independent physical mechanism.
this_is_not: not a rejection of the mathematical sine-kernel claim
not a rejection of visual analogy

decision.reason: Use “diffraction-like kernel visualization,” not “literal diffraction.”
decision.next_step: Enforce in all downstream text.

next_step_idea: Add this as a required wording rule in MATH_R14_A.

title: Collision · probability as accounting vs theorem
one_line: “Probability is brightness/accounting” is an interpretation; theorems like Gleason constrain measures but do not by themselves prove R14 ontology.
this_is_not: not a rejection of Born/Gleason anchors
not a rejection of probability-as-geometry lens

decision.reason: Treat as CCT interpretation until math says more.
decision.next_step: Ask future pass to separate Born-rule theorem, Gleason theorem, and R14 metaphor.

next_step_idea: Do not let this sentence appear in abstract/paper.

title: Collision · source recovery is ill-posed
one_line: R14 must not aim to recover the hidden source/origin; at most it can recover equivalence classes and projection invariants.
this_is_not: not a claim that inverse problems are always impossible
not a reason to stop computing invariants

decision.reason: This prevents R14 from becoming a universal solvent.
decision.next_step: Use “recover invariants, not source” in next-agent prompt.

next_step_idea: Make this the first line of R14 methodology.

title: Collision · time as render axis
one_line: If using block-universe / spacetime-in-picture framing, the render axis should be scale/RG-depth, not ordinary time.
this_is: a normalization of Phi’s own correction
this_is_not: not proof that RG is fundamental time
not proof that time is unreal
not a general replacement for temporal dynamics

decision.reason: Useful as hypothesis wording, not as theorem.
decision.next_step: If used, state “scale-like render axis in this analogy.”

next_step_idea: Route to R02/R06 later, not first compute.

title: RUN-0002 low-temperature verdict
one_line: R14 survives only as a synthesis probe. Promote R14-A and R14-C to first source-audit/Colab lane; freeze the rest.

not publication approval
not rejection of other branches
